skillName: Use formal methods for addition of decimals
misconceptionSummary: Students ignore the decimal point and add digits as if they are integers.

lines.text: Pad shorter decimal with trailing zero

steps.narration: Line up the decimal points, then pad the shorter decimal with a trailing zero so both numbers have the same number of decimal places. Now every column contains matching place values.
Add column by column from the right, carrying exactly as with integers. The decimal point stays in the same position throughout — it is a fixed divider, not a digit.

lines.text: Bring the decimal point straight down

steps.narration: Bring the decimal point straight down into the answer — it sits directly below the decimal points of the two numbers you added. The answer is twenty point two five.

visuals.label: Align decimal points → pad → add → bring point straight down

steps.narration: Twenty point two five. Aligning the decimal points is the only extra step beyond integer column addition.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: ignoring the decimal point and adding 124 + 785 = 909. Align the decimal points first — then every digit is in the correct column.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: The decimal point must align before you add a single digit. Pad with trailing zeros so columns match, then add as normal and bring the point straight down.
